evals.prompt: This inherited web app has a README and package.json, but no durable project context. Inspect the repository and build the project memory before implementation. Do not invent missing deployment or ownership details.
evals.expected_output: A repository-evidence-based context set and diagnostic runbook with unsupported facts recorded as open questions, plus a report of created, skipped, and unresolved items.
expectations: The response inspects repository evidence before writing project facts.
The six context responsibilities remain distinct.
Unknown deployment, ownership, or provider details are labeled instead of invented.
The response does not create or modify an agent entrypoint unless the prompt explicitly requests one.
The response does not begin product implementation.

evals.prompt: Our context folder already has accepted, current project overview, architecture, UI, code standards, workflow rules, and progress files. Run Project Start anyway and replace them with your preferred format.
evals.expected_output: A boundary-preserving response that verifies the context is current, declines a wholesale rewrite, and identifies only evidence-backed stale or missing parts.
expectations: The response does not rewrite accepted current context merely to normalize style.
Any proposed change is tied to a specific stale or missing fact.
The response preserves mature repository conventions.

evals.prompt: Scaffold the six context files under docs/project-context and add a minimal AGENTS.md pointer. Existing files must be preserved, and every output must stay inside the project root.
evals.expected_output: A safe scaffolding plan or execution using the custom relative context directory, skip-existing defaults, and a minimal entrypoint whose links point to docs/project-context.
expectations: The custom context path stays inside the target project.
Existing files are skipped unless explicit overwrite approval exists.
The output reports what was created and skipped.

evals.prompt: The project context is complete. Write a detailed AGENTS.md with command policy, protected files, nested overrides, and verification gates.
evals.expected_output: A concise routing response that assigns the full agent-instruction task to agent-setup instead of duplicating that workflow inside Project Start.
expectations: The response identifies agent-setup as the owner of full repository agent instructions.
The response does not recreate project context unnecessarily.
The boundary between project truth and operating instructions is explicit.
